understanding.goal_understanding: The goal was to navigate a tile grid and reach the goal gem tile marked '+'. Each level required finding a path through the grid using horizontal movement and gravity-based falling, clicking breakable blocks to open new paths, while avoiding spike tiles that would end the run.
understanding.mechanics_understanding: Movement was horizontal only (MOVE_LEFT/MOVE_RIGHT), after which gravity would pull the player in the current gravity direction until landing on a solid tile. In this level gravity was DOWN, meaning after each horizontal move the player would fall downward until hitting a solid tile. CLICK actions removed breakable 'x' tiles at specified coordinates, which could open fall paths - importantly, clicking a block directly below the player would cause them to fall through it to the next surface. Clicks did not advance turn count in the same way as moves (though they did consume steps). The structured state provided left_landing and right_landing previews showing where the player would end up after gravity resolved. Spikes (u/v tiles) were instant-kill hazards. UNDO could revert the previous action. The level had a 50-turn budget per attempt with 2 attempts available.
